items.title: The Breakfast Battle: Convenience Stores Are Taking a Bite Out of Fast-Food Chains' Morning Revenues
items.description: Food-forward convenience stores are becoming a morning staple for a growing number of consumers, market research firm Circana finds. According to the company's U.S. C-Store Landscape Q2 2025 report, morning meal trips at convenience stores were up by 1% over the last three-month period. Breakfast trips now account for 25% of all convenience store food purchases. "Over the long run, convenience stores have taken share, really at foodservice overall, but the morning meal has been their strong suit

items.title: Ben & Jerry's Co-Founder Resigns In Protest As Politics Continue To Influence Corporate Exits
items.description: Jerry Greenfield, of Ben & Jerry's fame, didn't resign over personal controversy—he quit because his company stopped being political enough.

items.title: McDonald’s Corporation’s (MCD) Dividend History: A Reliable Addition to Any Dividend Stock Portfolio
items.description: McDonald’s Corporation (NYSE:MCD) is included among the Best Stocks for a Dividend Stock Portfolio. McDonald’s Corporation (NYSE:MCD) is an American multinational fast food company. With a largely franchised model and strong operating margins, the company is able to turn a significant portion of its revenue into profit and cash flow, funding both dividends and share […]
